title: Authentication, Sessions, Passwords, Bearer Tokens, and Credential Recovery · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A server pairing session predicts request, handler, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before building the first authentication and credential lifecycle policy for authentication, sessions, passwords, bearer tokens, and credential recovery. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of authentication and credential lifecycle policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Separate presented credential, verification, authenticated principal, session, assurance, freshness, and downstream authorization.
Use reviewed password hashing, bounded comparison, generic failures, rate and abuse controls, MFA or step-up policy, and secure recovery lifecycle.
Issue opaque sessions with rotation, server-side state or revocation, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Path, Domain, expiry, fixation defense, and logout invalidation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Authn Proof Principal Session
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 5451 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate token claims and session rotation before constructing a principal; then return a five-hundred response and retain a low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Treat bearer access tokens as scoped resource-server credentials under RFC 6750 and BCP 9700 without implementing an authorization server casually.
